top_patterns: carried amount named as one instead of ten

evidence_samples.source_title: Children's understanding of conventional and logical rules in multidigit addition and subtraction
evidence_samples.source_location: JMB notebook source surfaced by NotebookLM, p. 299
evidence_samples.support_pattern: carried amount named as one instead of ten
evidence_samples.support_summary: NotebookLM surfaced an interview finding where half of the addition students described the carried amount as one rather than ten, disconnecting the carry mark from its base-ten value.

evidence_samples.source_title: Unpacking Pedagogical Content Knowledge
evidence_samples.source_location: JRME notebook source surfaced by NotebookLM, pp. 380-381, 389-390
evidence_samples.support_pattern: always carry 1 when column sum needs 2 tens
evidence_samples.support_summary: NotebookLM surfaced a multidigit-addition diagnostic item where the same error was carrying one ten instead of two tens after a column sum of twenty or more.

evidence_samples.source_location: JMB notebook source surfaced by NotebookLM, pp. 226, 233
evidence_samples.support_pattern: regrouped digit lacks reference unit
evidence_samples.support_summary: NotebookLM surfaced preservice-teacher evidence that carried digits can be treated as disconnected symbols rather than coordinated one-ten or one-hundred reference units.

evidence_samples.source_title: A research-inspired and computer-guided clinical interview for mathematics assessment: introduction, reliability and validity
evidence_samples.source_location: ZDM notebook source surfaced by NotebookLM, p. 1010
evidence_samples.support_pattern: 99 + 1 becomes 910 by appending raw column sum
evidence_samples.support_summary: NotebookLM surfaced Ginsburg et al.'s diagnostic example where a student fails to carry and writes the unregrouped ones-column sum next to the tens digit, producing 910 for 99 + 1.

evidence_samples.source_title: Understanding place value with numeration units
evidence_samples.source_location: ZDM notebook source surfaced by NotebookLM, Table 4 and Section 5.1.1
evidence_samples.support_pattern: raw numeration-unit totals juxtaposed as 31215
evidence_samples.support_summary: NotebookLM surfaced Houdement and Tempier as direct evidence that students may combine each base-ten unit separately and then juxtapose over-capacity unit counts rather than converting twelve hundreds into one thousand and two hundreds.

evidence_samples.source_title: Understanding place value with numeration units

practices.description: Recall a base division fact and scale the quotient by a power of ten.
Diagnostic validation by distributive decomposition: validate a product by summing partial products.
Diagnostic validation across two candidate estimates: compare their omitted partial products and select the smaller-omission estimate.
Diagnostic validation by inverse multiplication: reject the candidate quotient when divisor * proposed exceeds dividend.
Diagnostic cardinality: establish a collection's cardinal by bijective counting under the five counting principles.
Diagnostic justification: re-ground a procedural fraction rule by invoking the area-model kernel on the same inputs.

evidence_samples.support_pattern: compute both products instead of recognizing commuted equivalence
evidence_samples.support_summary: NotebookLM surfaced Kaminski as direct evidence that students decided whether 24 x 27 x 3 and 3 x 27 x 24 were equivalent by computing both sides and comparing totals instead of using commutativity structurally.

evidence_samples.source_title: Students' conceptualisations of multiplication as repeated addition or equal groups in relation to multi-digit and decimal numbers
evidence_samples.source_authors: K. Larsson, K. Pettersson, and P. Andrews
evidence_samples.source_location: JMB notebook source surfaced by NotebookLM, pp. 11-12
evidence_samples.support_pattern: fixed factor-role model constrains commutativity
evidence_samples.support_summary: NotebookLM surfaced Larsson et al. as direct evidence that students with asymmetric equal-groups or repeated-addition models may waver over factor-order reversal and must consciously override fixed multiplier/multiplicand roles.

evidence_samples.source_title: Teacher Education Students' Number Sense: Initial Explorations
evidence_samples.source_location: MERJ notebook source surfaced by NotebookLM, p. 228
evidence_samples.support_pattern: fixed factor-role repeated addition blocks factor reversal
evidence_samples.support_summary: NotebookLM surfaced Kaminski as direct evidence that students asked to solve 32 x 24 by repeated addition tended to preserve the given factor roles by iterating 24 thirty-two times rather than using the commuted repeated-addition alternative.

evidence_samples.source_title: Overcoming a 4th grader's challenges with working-memory via a constructivist-based pedagogy and strategic scaffolds: Tia's solutions to challenging multiplicative tasks
evidence_samples.source_authors: C. Hord, R. Tzur, Y.P. Xin, L. Si, R.H. Kenney, and J. Woodward
evidence_samples.source_location: JMB notebook source surfaced by NotebookLM, p. 18
evidence_samples.support_pattern: bags plus marbles added instead of iterating bags of marbles
evidence_samples.support_summary: NotebookLM surfaced Tia's equal-groups marble-bag task as direct evidence that a learner can answer 6 bags with 3 marbles each as 9 by adding the group count and the items-per-group count instead of coordinating bags as composite units.

evidence_samples.source_title: A Developmental Multimodal Model for Multiplication and Division
evidence_samples.source_location: MERJ notebook source surfaced by NotebookLM, p. 72
evidence_samples.support_pattern: multiplier and multiplicand modeled as separate counts and added
evidence_samples.support_summary: NotebookLM surfaced Mulligan and Watson as direct developmental evidence that learners who have not formed a composite multiplicative unit may extract two quantities from a multiplication task, model them as independent counts, and add them to get 7.
